result:
All fixes verified with a real production build and local server. Summary of what changed:

**Fixed (all in the source repo, `results/.../workspace`):**
1. **`app/layout.tsx`** — replaced the leftover "InboxZap" title/description/OG/Twitter tags with real Lumina metadata, added `metadataBase`, and added `SoftwareApplication` JSON-LD.
2. **`app/features/page.tsx`** and **`app/pricing/page.tsx`** (new) — these were linked in nav and the sitemap but didn't exist (404s). Built real content pages with their own metadata.
3. **`app/page.tsx`** — added a real `#faq` section (the `/faq` redirect pointed at a section that never existed) plus `FAQPage` JSON-LD.
4. **Blog rebuild** — the old blog fetched `/api/posts` client-side, but that route never existed, so it always showed "Could not load posts" to users and crawlers alike. Replaced with `app/blog/posts.ts` (6 posts on real Lumina-relevant topics: AI meeting notes, action items, async meetings, meeting minutes, platform coverage, cost of bad notes) rendered server-side at `/blog` and `/blog/[slug]`, each with its own metadata and `BlogPosting` JSON-LD.
5. **`next.config.js`** — added 301s from the old, now-orphaned `/blog/post-1`…`post-12` URLs (still indexed per GSC, all about the discontinued InboxZap email-cleanup product) to `/blog`.
6. **`app/sitemap.ts`** — now includes `/features`, `/pricing`, and every blog post; previously only listed 4 static URLs, two of which 404'd.
7. **`app/opengraph-image.tsx`** (new) — the referenced `/img/og-inboxzap.png` didn't exist in `public/`; added a dynamically generated OG image instead.

Verified via `npm run build` (compiles clean, all routes prerender) and a local `next start` on port 3100: homepage now serves correct title/meta/OG, `/features` and `/pricing` return 200, `/blog` server-renders all 6 posts without JS, `/faq` and old `/blog/post-N` URLs redirect correctly, and the sitemap lists every real, live URL.

One unrelated note: `npm install` flagged that `next@14.2.5` has a known security advisory — not an SEO issue, but worth a dependency bump when convenient.
